rows.statusLabel: proved Paper D v12 first-grid cap
rows.source: Paper D v12 first-grid deep-point cap
nonClaims: Applies to official-rate rows with 127 <= k <= 2^40, q<2^256, and q>n; it is stronger than the older fixed cap 255/512 in that range.

rows.title: Prime-field Paper D near-capacity cap, rho=1/2

prizeBox.quotientDivisor: cap divisor N=n (first closed grid point)

rows.title: CAP25 v13 raw KoalaBear identity-prefix edge

prizeBox.quotientDivisor: identity-prefix scale c=1 over the deployed KoalaBear subgroup

rows.source: CAP25 v13 raw corrected identity frontier
nonClaims: The exact v13 raw proposition proves the MCA unsafe edge at agreement 1116047; this is a delta* upper cap, not an exact threshold.
The identity-prefix lower comparison fails at 1116048 by 22.20 bits, but that only leaves 1116048 open; it does not prove safety there.
The KoalaBear list row is separate and remains at 1116046 unsafe / 1116047 open.
This v13 raw result is not promoted here into the active exact-threshold manuscript.

rows.title: Interleaved-list δ* upper cap from base-list obstruction
rows.qGen: generated field of the smooth row
nonClaims: List lower bound only; it is not by itself an MCA or protocol soundness theorem.
This is a δ*_list upper cap from a list-size lower bound, not an exact threshold.

rows.title: Hybrid quotient-residual interleaved-list lower row

prizeBox.quotientDivisor: quotient map x -> x^32; fiber size 32; quotient size 16

nonClaims: Interleaved-list lower bound only; not an MCA N_bad row.
Does not claim protocol soundness, ordinary list-decoding exactness, or exact delta*_C.
The +0.009 bit margin is a narrow denominator gate: seven witnesses beat floor(17^32/2^128)=6.

rows.title: Prime192 Paper D v12 instantiated cap row
nonClaims: Instantiated example of the Paper D v12 cap, not a new theorem beyond Paper D.
Paper D v12 cap status applies after the scanner verifies the divisor, binomial, and field ledgers.
The bad-count field is an equivalent numerator from the MCA error-floor lower bound, not an explicit slope census.

rows.title: F_17^32 Paper D v12 instantiated cap row
nonClaims: Instantiated example of the Paper D v12 cap, not a new theorem beyond Paper D.

rows.title: Random simple-pole finite-slope floors
nonClaims: Finite-slope support-wise lower floors only; finite slopes are sampled over q_line=17^32.
The displayed agreement rows are separate existence statements, not one line realizing all rows simultaneously.
Not a safe-side bound, not ordinary list decoding, and not protocol soundness.

rows.title: Coset-packet finite-slope floors
nonClaims: Finite-slope support-wise lower floors only; not a safe-side theorem.
Rows are separate existence statements and not an exact Bad_fin classification.
Not ordinary list decoding, interleaved list size, or protocol soundness.

rows.title: Tangent lower-floor capacity-edge row
nonClaims: Finite-slope support-wise MCA lower floor only.
Not an exact threshold.
Not ordinary list decoding or protocol soundness.

rows.title: Cycle116 closed-threshold proof record
rows.source: Cycle120 scope note plus tangent gate audit
nonClaims: The >2^-128 gate is unconditional via the tangent floor: LD_sw(C,262) >= 251 >= 7.
The exact 52,747,567,092 numerator and +32.82 bit margin still import the Cycle84 finite count.
Source-scoped finite row; not ordinary list decoding, protocol soundness, or an exact threshold.

rows.title: Cycle119 strict263 proof record
rows.source: Cycle119 review plus tangent gate audit
nonClaims: The strict >2^-128 gate is unconditional via the tangent floor: LD_sw(C,263) >= 250 >= 7.
The exact 52,747,567,092 numerator and +32.82 bit margin still import the Cycle84 finite count.
Finite/source-scoped; not ordinary list decoding or an official prize solve by itself.

rows.title: Strict264 quotient-floor proof record
nonClaims: Quotient-core mechanism record, not the active threshold pin.
Superseded as a lower-bound frontier by the generic tangent floor, which gives LD_sw(C,264) >= 249.

rows.title: Tangent-star exact finite-slope gate
nonClaims: Finite-slope support-wise MCA convention only.
Adjacent high-agreement CA/projective/curve/list ledgers are recorded separately.
Protocol, folding, query, extension-lift, and cryptographic losses require separate ledgers.

rows.title: Prime-field A425/A426 adjacent finite-slope gate

prizeBox.quotientDivisor: p == 1 mod 512, so F_p^* has an order-512 subgroup

rows.source: PR #204 A426 two-core exact threshold packet
nonClaims: Finite-slope support-wise MCA / LD_sw object only.
This is a separate prime-field row, not the F_17^32 row.
Not ordinary list decoding, not protocol soundness, and not an efficient attack claim.

rows.title: Prime-field A406/A407 adjacent finite-slope gate

prizeBox.quotientDivisor: p == 1 mod 512, so F_p^* has an order-512 subgroup

nonClaims: Finite-slope support-wise MCA / LD_sw only.
LD_sw(C,406) is not classified exactly; only the tangent lower floor >=107 is used at the unsafe endpoint.
This is a separate engineered prime-field row, not the F_17^32 or KoalaBear row.
Not ordinary list decoding, interleaved-list safety, protocol soundness, or full M1 closure.

rows.title: Strict352 quotient-core proof record
nonClaims: Quotient-core mechanism record, not the active lower-bound frontier.
The generic tangent floor gives LD_sw(C,352) >= 161 and LD_sw(C,353) >= 160.

rows.title: Strict264 2187-slope candidate
nonClaims: Candidate shape only; requires an independent theorem and admissibility audit.
